channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Dave Kendall, MTV's 120 Minutes Creator, Passes Away, Leaving Legacy in Music Journalism
item.description: Dave Kendall, the creator and host of MTV's iconic music video show 120 Minutes, has passed away. His former colleague Matt Pinfield announced the news on social media, noting that Kendall's death mar...

item.title: Tanzanian Government Halts Construction at Uhuru Park Over Public Land Concerns
item.description: The Tanzanian government has stopped construction activities at Uhuru Park in Moshi, a public space with historical significance. This decision comes after local residents and civic groups raised conc...

item.title: Toronto FC and CF Montreal End MLS Resumption Match in 0-0 Draw
item.description: Toronto FC and CF Montréal delivered a scoreless draw in their MLS resumption match, with both teams struggling to break the deadlock. Despite Montreal dominating the shot count 15-7, including 3-0 on...

item.title: Taslima Nasrin's Return to Kolkata and the Political Stalemate in Bangladesh
item.description: Taslima Nasrin, the Bangladeshi-born writer, is set to return to Kolkata after nearly two decades in exile. Her planned visit to Rabindra Sadan on August 1 marks her first return to the city since 200...

item.title: Nairobi Introduces New Development Policy to Regulate Urban Growth
item.description: Nairobi City County has officially gazetted the Development Control Policy 2026, marking a major step in Governor Johnson Sakaja's efforts to manage the city's rapid urban expansion. The policy introd...

item.title: Stormers XXIII Launch Currie Cup Campaign Against Griquas in New Era
item.description: The Stormers XXIII, rebranded from the traditional Western Province team, kick off their Currie Cup campaign with a match against defending champions Griquas on Friday evening at DHL Stadium. Veteran ...

item.title: Court of Appeal strikes down Electoral Act provisions on party primaries and membership registers
item.description: The Court of Appeal in Abuja has ruled that sections 77(5), (6), (7) and 84(2) of the Electoral Act 2026 are unconstitutional. These provisions, which required political parties to submit membership r...

item.title: iran and us escalate gulf tensions with mutual attacks
item.description: iran has launched fresh attacks on us military facilities in the gulf following a sixth consecutive day of us strikes on iranian targets. the us military stated that its airstrikes aimed to degrade ir...

item.title: Thai Socialite Aon Somrutai's Manila Visit and Fashion Outfits
item.description: Thai socialite Aon Somrutai has arrived in Manila, sparking interest among fashion enthusiasts. The article highlights her various outfits and appearances during her stay, showcasing her style and fas...

item.title: Phoebe Bridgers performs acoustic version of 'Lost Boys' on 'The Tonight Show' and announces new album
item.description: Phoebe Bridgers returned to 'The Tonight Show Starring Jimmy Fallon' for a stripped-back acoustic rendition of her single 'Lost Boys', following its television debut the previous night. The performanc...

item.title: Fatima Oloko's Rise: NWFL Star Shines at WAFCON 2026
item.description: Fatima Oloko, 18, has become a beacon of hope for Nigerian women's football after her meteoric rise from being an unused goalkeeper at Rivers Angels to being a key player in Nigeria's WAFCON 2026 squa...

item.title: US Imposes New Visa Limits on Students and Journalists
item.description: The US government has introduced new rules that will limit the duration of student and journalist visas. Under the policy, foreign students will now be allowed to stay for the length of their academic...

item.title: Senior Counsel Advises Ruto to Avoid Mt Kenya Running Mate After UDA's By-Election Loss
item.description: Senior Counsel Ahmednasir Abdullahi has urged President William Ruto to abandon plans for a Mt Kenya running mate following the United Democratic Alliance (UDA)'s defeat in the Ol Kalou by-election. T...

item.title: Pretty Woman the Musical: A Nostalgic Take with Mixed Results
item.description: The review of *Pretty Woman: The Musical* highlights its reliance on nostalgia for the iconic 1990 film, while critiquing its adaptation challenges. The musical, set in a world of conspicuous consumpt...

item.title: Emmerdale actor Bradley Riches shares details of theft during Heartstopper premiere incident
item.description: Bradley Riches, a cast member of the British soap opera Emmerdale, has disclosed that he experienced a robbery while attending the premiere of the popular coming-of-age series Heartstopper. The incide...

item.title: Coca-Cola Halts Fairlife Dairy Production After Ransomware Attack
item.description: Coca-Cola, a global beverage giant, has temporarily suspended production at its Fairlife dairy subsidiary following a ransomware attack that disrupted its operations. The company disclosed the inciden...

item.title: Venezuelan Coastal Town Remains Unscathed After Earthquake
item.description: A coastal town in Venezuela, referred to as 'blessed,' has miraculously avoided significant damage from a recent earthquake. The incident occurred on July 17, 2026, and highlights the region's vulnera...

item.title: Two Wildlife-Related Deaths Under Investigation Near La Ronge, Saskatchewan
item.description: Two deaths at McTavish Lake, located north of La Ronge in Saskatchewan, are suspected to be wildlife-related, according to the Saskatchewan government. Conservation officers and the RCMP are investiga...

item.title: Cape Town's Good Hope Centre sale sparks political controversy over transparency and housing
item.description: The sale of Cape Town's Good Hope Centre has become a flashpoint in local politics, with the GOOD Party accusing Mayor Geordin Hill-Lewis of mishandling the multimillion-rand transaction. The controve...

item.title: Mark Carney Justifies New Pipeline Amid Climate Concerns and Wildfire Challenges
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney has defended his government's decision to proceed with a new oil pipeline project despite growing scrutiny over Canada's climate commitments and ongoing wildfires in norther...
